# Knowledge base — index for clinicians and researchers

These are **analysis notes, not owner material.** They are written in English,
they carry verbatim source excerpts, and they state explicitly where the
evidence runs out. For owner-facing material see [`../guides/`](../guides/) or
the [published site](https://polyketide.github.io/catmed/).

**12 files, 150 papers, 562 verbatim excerpts**, every one
re-checked byte-for-byte against its archived PubMed record on every commit.

## If you are a veterinarian or a researcher

**The most useful thing you can do here is tell us something is wrong.** Three
issue templates exist for exactly that:

| | For |
|---|---|
| [Challenge a figure](../../../issues/new?template=01-challenge-a-figure.yml) | a number here is wrong, misattributed, or overstated |
| [Clinical review](../../../issues/new?template=02-clinical-review.yml) | the reading is defensible on paper and wrong in practice |
| [Propose coverage](../../../issues/new?template=03-propose-coverage.yml) | a condition owners keep facing that this does not cover |

**Clinical experience without a citation is welcome.** How it is recorded —
without either dismissing it or laundering it into evidence it is not — is set
out in [`../docs/LITERATURE-PIPELINE-SOP.md`](../docs/LITERATURE-PIPELINE-SOP.md) §10.

The **⚠️ Stated gap** lines below are where this project has already searched
and found nothing. Those are the fastest places for your knowledge to be worth
more than anything written here.

### [`chronic-kidney-disease.md`](chronic-kidney-disease.md)

**Feline Chronic Kidney Disease: What Is Established, What Is Only Predicted, and What an Owner Decides**

CKD is 25× more common than the lymphoma this repository was built around, is roughly a tenth of all feline deaths, and its evidence is split across nephrology, nutrition, endocrinology and hypertension literatures — an integration gap,…

*11 papers, 46 verbatim excerpts.*

### [`feline-disease-frequency.md`](feline-disease-frequency.md)

**What Cats Actually Present With, and What They Die Of**

this repository chose its topics from one cat's illness. Before choosing the next one it needed evidence about what cat owners actually face, rather than an assumption. The assumption turned out to be right in direction and badly wrong…

*7 papers, 28 verbatim excerpts.*

### [`feline-hypertension.md`](feline-hypertension.md)

**Systemic Hypertension in Cats — the disease that is only found after it does damage**

hypertension is the third member of the older-cat triad alongside chronic kidney disease and hyperthyroidism (hyperthyroidism-and-kidney-disease.md), and the only one that is *silent*. It produces no signs an owner can notice until it…

*5 papers, 47 verbatim excerpts.*

> ⚠️ **Stated gap:** Whether earlier detection of hypertension changes outcomes, as opposed to detecting damage earlier. Nothing retrieved tests this, and it is the same u…

### [`hyperthyroidism-and-kidney-disease.md`](hyperthyroidism-and-kidney-disease.md)

**Hyperthyroidism and Kidney Disease in Cats — two diseases that hide each other**

these are the two commonest chronic diseases of older cats, they occur together, and each one *conceals* the other. An endocrine specialist watching thyroid hormone and a nephrologist watching creatinine can both be reading their own…

*3 papers, 16 verbatim excerpts.*  ·  **中文版: [`hyperthyroidism-and-kidney-disease.zh.md`](hyperthyroidism-and-kidney-disease.zh.md)**

> ⚠️ **Stated gap:** Whether earlier detection of masked CKD changes outcomes, rather than merely predicting them. No study here tests that.
